title: Old Tunes
content:
آ آ آ آ When friends are listening round me, Jack, to hear my dying breath,
آ آ آ آ And I am lying in a sleep they say will end in death,
آ آ آ آ Donï؟½t notice what the doctor says, and let the nurse complain,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll tell you how to rouse me if Iï؟½ll ever wake again.

آ آ آ آ Just you bring in your fiddle, Jack, and set your heart in tune,
آ آ آ آ And strike up ï؟½Annie Laurieï؟½, or ï؟½The Rising of the Moonï؟½;
آ آ آ آ And if you see no token of a rising in my throat,
آ آ آ آ Youï؟½ll need to brace your mouth, old manï؟½Iï؟½m booked by Charonï؟½s boat.

آ آ آ آ And if you are not satisfied that I am off the scene,
آ آ آ آ Strike up ï؟½The Marseillaiseï؟½, or else ï؟½The Wearing of the Greenï؟½;
آ آ آ آ And should my fingers tremble not, then I have crossed the line,
آ آ آ آ But keep your fingers steady, Jack, and strike up ï؟½Auld Lang Syneï؟½.
meaning: About: Henry Lawson (17 June 1867 - 2 September 1922) was an Australian writer and poet. Along with his contemporary Banjo Paterson, Lawson is among the best-known Australian poets and fiction writers of the colonial period.
